issues.title: Missing Return Statement in Cluster Bean Example in Couchbase Documentation
issues.body:
**Bug description**
In the ```spring-ai-docs repository```, within the file ```src/main/antora/modules/ROOT/pages/api/vectordbs/couchbase.adoc```, the provided Java code snippet for manually configuring the Couchbase Cluster bean is **missing a return** statement. This results in a **compilation error** due to the method not returning a Cluster instance. Ensuring accurate code snippets in documentation is crucial for developers referencing them. Including the return statement aligns with proper Java practices and prevents potential confusion.

**Environment**
Spring AI version : 0.8.0
Java version : 17 
Vector store: Couchbase

**Steps to reproduce**

1. Copy the provided cluster() method from the documentation into a Spring Boot application.
2. Attempt to compile the application.


**Expected behavior**
The application should compile successfully with the cluster() method returning a valid Cluster instance.

**Minimal Complete Reproducible example**
The current code in the documentation:

```java
@Bean
public Cluster cluster() {
    Cluster cluster = Cluster.connect("couchbase://localhost", "username", "password");
}
```
This will result in a **missing return statement error**.

**Proposed Fix**
To fix this issue, the method should explicitly return the Cluster instance:
```java
@Bean
public Cluster cluster() {
    return Cluster.connect("couchbase://localhost", "username", "password");
}
```

issues.title: Problems with DefaultTools overloading, and SyncMcpToolCallbackProvider cannot be injected automatically
issues.body:
Env: 
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/38f69b24-e752-47ed-9cec-c048d487b1e7)

When I configure the MCP client side using the Auto configuration, only List < McpClients > can find the bean and inject it; but SyncMcpToolCallbackProvider shows that the bean class is not found, unlike a reference archive (although ToolCallBackProvider types of beans can be injected).

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9cc6112f-940b-43e4-8bc5-0f156e860117)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/24b89bbe-c3a3-4e36-839f-50e13705dbce)

In addition, if I use mcpclient's listTools () directly, the overload of .defaultTools() will also choose the parameter Object, causing the tool to fail.
The solution is that .defaultTools ()/.tools () must pass parameters of the form ToolCallback

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f311b3c2-beac-4689-8105-1819d10fe8d8)

comments.body:
Ran into this as well in M7. Docs seem wrong:
https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/1.0/api/mcp/mcp-client-boot-starter-docs.html#_usage_example

Also IntelliJ can't resolve the `spring.ai.mcp.client.toolcallback.enabled` property.
I also ran into same issue with version M7
Another issue reporting the same problem: https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/issues/2495

> Another issue reporting the same problem: [#2495](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/issues/2495)

Thanks for pointing that out! I initially ran into the missing callback issue when passing `McpClient.listTools()` directly as the `defaultTools` parameter. But yes, it's effectively the same as #2495

> I like the proposed solution.

ditto
method name changes for tools is done and added info to the upgrade-notes.adoc  merged in 091fca2d163e757394f5b2a68d87934fc3388647

issues.title: Writing according to the official documentation's example for Tools in version 1.0.0-M6 does not trigger the custom tool.

comments.body:
> May be you can try constructing a prompt from the tools and see if that works.
> 
> ```
> ToolCallingChatOptions chatOptions = ToolCallingChatOptions.builder()
>             .toolCallbacks(tools)
>             .build();
> Prompt prompt = new Prompt(text, chatOptions);
> return chatClient.prompt(prompt)
>     .call()
>     .content();
> ```
> 
> Also try 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT

Thanks for your suggestion. After trying it, it still doesn't work.

I'm using it like that and it works fine for me. Are you sure the LLM is actually making a tool call? You can either use the SimpleLoggerAdvisor or if you want to log the actual Rest level request/response you can see the log solution I posted here https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/issues/883#issuecomment-2739772323
Thank you for your suggestion! I found that the issue might be with the **gpt-3.5-turbo** model. After switching to the latest models, everything is working fine.
